title: Real-Robot Lab Infra
description: Engineering stack for teleoperation, data collection, and robot-side validation
summary: This is the engineering base that keeps weekly research work grounded in real data collection, linked hardware assets/resources, and real-robot validation.
asset_caption: Hardware matrix: ready systems, dual-Franka same-computer control, and planned glove / tracker teleoperation stack.
details: Ready inventory baseline: Linker FFG, UDCAP data glove, Cobot Magic / Piper-X, Linker Hand L6, and Daimon UMI.

Missing wrapper: Linker TA + Linker FFG still needs a local control wrapper before it can be treated as ready infra. The wrapper should expose connection/config, state readback, command open/close or position/force targets, error handling, and a smoke verifier.
Completed today: ARX / Fangzhou Wuxian real-robot infra setup.

ARX SOP received: Operation of ARX Robot Arm for Drag Teaching and Teleoperation Data Collection, JZ/SOP/ROB/001, issued 2026-05-23. It covers trained-operator responsibility, power-up, drag teaching, teleoperation, shutdown, and immediate stop conditions.
Progress on 2026-05-18: dual Franka arms can now be controlled from the same computer.
Progress on 2026-05-21: Gello-controlled dual Franka + Wuji Hand setup is complete. Current control treats the Wuji dexterous hand as a one-DoF gripper via Gello trigger open/close mapping.
Demo waterproofing consumables purchased: disposable nitrile gloves and finger cots for short demo protection.

DexGello now owns the MANUS + VIVE research input stack: infra keeps the completed Franka + Wuji + GELLO base and hardware availability, while DexGello tracks the decoupled q_arm / T_hand / q_finger verifier and dataset logger.
Before Feibo uses the Franka dexterous-hand setup for egg-cracking tests, cable-manage the setup with organizers / zip ties, clear an empty work area, and waterproof the outside area.

DexGello now owns the Wuji + Franka toy-knife slime-clay cutting demo and the Franka cuRobo planning migration. Real-Robot Infra should keep only shared hardware readiness and safety-gate support for those demos.
FACTR procurement route: purchase through Jingte Intelligent; keep the dashboard task focused on procurement path, gravity compensation, and force-feedback safety value.
Wrist tracking fallback: if VIVE Tracker is not used for wrist pose, evaluate an ACE Teleop-style wrist ring to replace the Gello-end trigger as the wrist / intent input device.
MANUS + VIVE merge update: the canonical verifier task has moved into DexGello because it is now part of the arm-hand-finger decoupled research system, not a generic infra checklist.
VIVE Tracker chest mount / strap purchase is complete and shipped, ETA 2026-06-16 to 2026-06-19 to COM2/NUS; use it to keep the CHEST tracker frame stable enough for calibration and replay.

VR teleoperation direction: implement a SteamVR/OpenVR input path for controlling Franka end-effector pose with controller / tracker / headset input, gated by calibration, workspace limits, velocity / acceleration caps, deadman switch, and E-stop readiness.

Canonical asset table linked: Real Robot Infra asset sheet gid 1134950065 contains 16 rows; key current assets include Franka Research 3, Wuji Hand, Wuji glove with D405 capture, YAM arms for ALOHA, D405 cameras, MANUS gloves, and VIVE trackers.

Hardware Resource Center is the canonical Notion target for manuals, SOPs, calibration notes, vendor PDFs, and hardware resource links; current connector access needs to be fixed before automated Notion content sync can verify page internals.
YAM ALOHA setup is grounded in asset LV-ROB-010: 数量:4；aloha系统, location E2-01-04, status 搭建中.

Wuji Glove is tracked as one canonical task: network smoke, timestamp logging, D405 capture wrapper, episode writer, and replay/loader verification are all acceptance items under task_real_robot_infra_wuji_glove.

ME Control Lab host expansion is now marked complete: at least two hosts have been added for the lab, resolving the previous one-host blocker for real-time control, data capture, monitoring, cache/storage, GPU/USB/NIC needs, account/network setup, bound devices, and smoke validation.
risks_decisions: 不同设备的驱动、SDK、ROS topic 和日志格式需要尽早统一，否则后面每条数据都会带维护成本。
手套、VIVE、相机、机器人状态要共享时间同步和外参标定策略。
真机 infra 必须保留安全边界：限位、急停、低速验证、碰撞恢复和操作记录；后续真机调试至少两人在场，一人专门看急停。
Gello 是被动主臂，软件无法在主臂端实现真正的重力补偿；主臂失手下落可能把 Franka 带向桌面，需要机械重力补偿、阻尼 / 刹车或 FACTR 这类带力反馈的双边遥操作方案。
一次性丁腈手套和指套已经买好，可用于短 demo 防水保护；但 Franka 灵巧手做敲鸡蛋等测试前，仍必须先处理杂线、固定线缆、清空工作区，并在工作区外做防水。

ARX drag-teaching / teleoperation collection must use the SOP as an operation gate: secure base, gripper, controller, power, cameras, and connections; clear the work area; test slow response first; keep people outside the workspace; stop immediately on abnormal resistance, sound, vibration, communication delay, unstable control, or unexpected motion.
工程状态需要和研究项目解耦：infra 卡片只追踪设备可用性，算法结果留在各项目卡片里。

DexGello demo safety: Wuji + Franka slime-clay cutting is no longer owned by Infra, but the hardware gate still requires toy knife first, fixture / dummy-material rehearsal, speed limits, blade-safe workspace, and explicit abort conditions; do not combine sharp-tool trials with single-person operation.
FACTR procurement route: FACTR purchase should go through Jingte Intelligent rather than the slow school process, while xArm / already-started school items remain separate.

VR teleoperation of Franka must be hardware-gated: start in simulation or read-only pose preview, then low-speed Cartesian control with workspace bounds, deadman switch, rate limiting, collision checks, and an E-stop operator before any live robot motion.
Every hardware TODO should cite its asset-table row and the corresponding Hardware Resource Center manual/SOP location before being marked done.
YAM ALOHA and Wuji glove capture both need a shared timestamp/schema path so their data can flow into the same real-robot dataset and replay verifier infrastructure.
Do not reopen separate Wuji Glove TODOs for network smoke or data wrapper; keep status, blockers, and evidence on the canonical Wuji Glove task.

DexGello boundary: Real Robot Infra should keep robot availability, safety, VR-control baseline, and purchase/setup work; DexGello should own research evidence for decoupled GELLO arm, VIVE hand pose, MANUS finger data, and demo-specific planning/control such as cuRobo.
ME Control Lab two-host expansion was completed on 2026-06-12; keep future host changes as separate scaling or maintenance TODOs rather than reopening the original two-host blocker.

external_resources.label: Hardware Resource Center
external_resources.notes: Canonical place for hardware manuals, SOPs, calibration docs, and vendor resources. Current Codex Notion connector could not fetch page contents; keep link as resource target and fix permissions if live sync is required.
